body:
# JSON REST API for user accounts and personal notes with on-disk persistence

## Context

Build a small, self-contained JSON REST API in Node.js for user accounts and
personal notes. The service is a multi-user system: authenticated users manage
notes that are private to them.

**Hard constraints (from the request):**

- **Runtime:** Node.js, **ESM** (`"type": "module"`), **no external web
  framework** — use the built-in `node:http` server (and standard library
  only for the HTTP/routing layer).
- **Persistence:** an **on-disk** store that survives a server restart — an
  embedded SQLite database file (`node:sqlite`, available in the Node.js
  runtime) or an equivalent on-disk store. **Not in-memory.** The DB file path
  should be configurable (e.g. via an env var) with a sensible default, and
  the file must be created on first run if absent.
- **Port:** listen on `process.env.PORT`, defaulting to `3000`.
- **npm `start` script:** `node <entrypoint>` so the server boots with
  `npm start`.

**API surface:**

| Method + path | Auth | Request body | Success response |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `POST /signup` | none | `{ "username", "password" }` | `201` `{ "id", "username" }` |
| `POST /login` | none | `{ "username", "password" }` | `200` `{ "session" }` |
| `GET /me` | bearer | — | `200` `{ "id", "username" }` |
| `POST /notes` | bearer | `{ "title", "body" }` | `201` the created note |
| `GET /notes` | bearer | — | `200` JSON array of the caller's notes |

**Auth model:** `POST /login` returns an opaque `session` credential. The
client sends it as a **bearer token** in the `Authorization: Bearer <session>`
header on the protected endpoints (`/me`, `POST /notes`, `GET /notes`). The
session token is server-generated, unguessable, and maps back to exactly one
user; sessions are persisted so they also survive a restart.

**Data isolation:** every note is owned by the user who created it. `GET /notes`
returns only the caller's own notes, and one user must never see or address
another user's notes.

**Passwords:** never store plaintext passwords. Store a salted hash using
Node's built-in `crypto` (e.g. `scrypt`) so credentials survive a restart and
`POST /login` can verify against the stored hash.

This is a cohesive, single-PR capability: one HTTP server module, one storage
module, one auth helper, wired together with a small router — see
[[test-driven-development]] for the endpoint-level test approach and
[[security-and-hardening]] for the auth/validation baseline.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm start` boots an HTTP server listening on `process.env.PORT`
      (default `3000`); the code uses only Node.js built-ins for HTTP and
      storage — no external web framework dependency in `package.json`.
- [ ] `POST /signup` with a JSON body `{ "username", "password" }` creates a
      user and responds with `{ "id", "username" }` (HTTP 201). The password is
      stored only as a salted hash, never plaintext.
- [ ] `POST /signup` rejects a duplicate username and a missing/blank
      `username` or `password` with a sensible `4xx` JSON error (409 for
      duplicate, 400 for malformed).
- [ ] `POST /login` with correct `{ "username", "password" }` responds with
      `{ "session": <opaque token> }` (HTTP 200); wrong credentials return
      `401` and do not leak whether the username exists.
- [ ] `GET /me` with a valid `Authorization: Bearer <session>` header returns
      the signed-in user's `{ "id", "username" }`; a missing/invalid/malformed
      token returns `401`.
- [ ] `POST /notes` with a valid session and `{ "title", "body" }` creates a
      note owned by the caller and returns the created note (HTTP 201,
      including its `id`); a malformed body returns `400` and no session
      returns `401`.
- [ ] `GET /notes` with a valid session returns a JSON array of **only** the
      caller's own notes; a second user with their own notes never sees the
      first user's notes (per-user isolation is verified by test).
- [ ] Users, notes, and sessions persist across a full server restart: after
      stopping and restarting the process against the same on-disk store, a
      previously created user can log in and previously created notes are
      still listed.
- [ ] Malformed input is handled sensibly across all endpoints — non-JSON
      bodies, wrong content, and unknown routes/methods return a JSON error
      with an appropriate `4xx`/`404`/`405` status rather than crashing the
      server.
- [ ] An automated test suite (`npm test`, via `node --test`) exercises the
      full flow — signup → login → `/me` → create note → list notes — plus the
      per-user isolation and restart-persistence cases.

## Out of Scope

- Any external web framework (Express, Fastify, Koa, etc.) or external HTTP
  router — the request explicitly forbids one.
- Session expiry / refresh, logout, password reset, email verification, or
  OAuth/third-party identity providers.
- Note update (`PUT`/`PATCH`) and delete (`DELETE`) endpoints, pagination,
  search, and sharing notes between users — only create and list are in scope.
- Rate limiting, CORS, TLS termination, and production deployment/packaging
  (containerization, process managers) — the deliverable is the API service
  itself.
- A frontend/UI, OpenAPI/Swagger spec generation, and structured request
  logging beyond basic error handling.

## Notes

- **Storage choice:** prefer the built-in `node:sqlite` module (a single DB
  file, real schema, cheap per-user queries) to satisfy "embedded SQLite …
  not in-memory" with zero dependencies. If the target Node version lacks
  `node:sqlite`, an equivalent zero-dependency on-disk store (e.g. an
  append-safe JSON/NDJSON file written atomically via `fs`) is acceptable so
  long as it survives a restart — document the choice and keep the storage
  layer behind a small module boundary so the rest of the code is
  store-agnostic.
- **Suggested schema:** `users(id, username UNIQUE, password_hash, salt)`,
  `notes(id, user_id, title, body, created_at)`, `sessions(token, user_id)`.
- **Session tokens:** generate with `crypto.randomBytes(...).toString('hex')`
  (or `crypto.randomUUID()`); treat the token as opaque and look the user up
  by it on each protected request.
- **Suggested layout:** an entrypoint (`server.js`) plus small modules for
  storage (`db.js`) and request helpers (JSON body parsing, bearer-token
  extraction, JSON responses). Keep the router a plain method+path switch.
- **DB file path** should be configurable (e.g. `DB_PATH` env var, default
  `./data.db` or `./data.sqlite`) and added to `.gitignore` so the store
  isn't committed.
- Follow the security baseline: validate/normalize all input, hash passwords,
  never log secrets or tokens, and return generic auth failures.
title: JSON REST API for user accounts and personal notes with on-disk persistence
